findings.title: New-user first-session chain: the biggest landmines are already filed as confirmed P0/P1 in their owning dimensions
findings.symptom: (Coverage note) Walking sign-in → first trip → days → AI plan → accept → to-dos → edit dates, the integration defects that actually bite a new user are: AI Accept Plan deleting the days they just journaled (ai · P0 confirmed), a friend's template-from-code losing imported to-dos (templates/journey-creator-template · P0 confirmed), and isCreator missing from the login response (auth · P2). No NEW standalone new-user-only integration defect surfaced beyond those.
findings.rootCause: The new-user journey is a composition of already-audited surfaces; its risks concentrate at the AI-accept and first-login seams, both already captured.
findings.suggestedFix: Fix the referenced P0s first; they all fire within a new user's first session and are the highest-leverage onboarding protections.

findings.title: First-run: brand-new user with zero trips can reach AI / Insights / Collections with no trip context
findings.symptom: Immediately after first login (no trips yet) a user can navigate to feature tabs that assume an active trip; empty states vary in quality across pages.
findings.rootCause: Routing does not funnel a zero-trip user to the create-first-trip flow; each page handles the no-trip case independently.
findings.repro: Sign in as a fresh account, before creating any trip open the AI / Insights tabs.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a single zero-trip onboarding gate (or consistent empty-state CTA) so the first action is always 'create your first trip'.
